edus.text: The Trump administration is moving to end asylum protections for most Central American migrants ,
the Departments of Justice and Homeland Security announced Monday .
According to text of the rule
set to publish in the Federal Register on Tuesday ,
asylum seekers
who pass through another country
before reaching the United States
will be ineligible for asylum
when they reach the southern border .
The move marks an acceleration in the Trump administration ' s efforts
to reduce the number of migrants
crossing the U.S. border with Mexico
and has the potential
to considerably reduce the number of asylum claims .
The Departments of Homeland Security and Justice announced the Interim Final Rule
( IFR )
in a joint statement Monday .
" The Departments are amending their respective regulations
to provide
that , with limited exceptions , an alien
who enters or attempts to enter the United States across the southern border
after failing to apply for protection in a third country outside the alien ' s country of citizenship , nationality , or last lawful habitual residence
through which the alien transited en route to the United States
is ineligible for asylum , "
states the text of the rule .
Under the rule , those
who have been the victims of trafficking
are granted exceptions .
The rule also allows exceptions for migrants
passing through countries
that have not signed major international refugee treaties
and for migrants
who have been denied asylum in the countries
they traveled through .
Trump administration officials described it as necessary to reduce the burden
placed on the U.S. by the growing number of immigrants
seeking asylum at the southern border
by allowing agencies to more quickly process cases
by weeding out individuals
trying to " exploit " the asylum protections .
" The United States is a generous country
but is being completely overwhelmed by the burdens
associated with apprehending and processing hundreds of thousands of aliens along the southern border , "

MORE
said in a statement .
" This Rule will decrease forum shopping by economic migrants and those
who seek to exploit our asylum system
to obtain entry to the United States
while ensuring that no one is removed from the United States
ho is more likely than not to be tortured or persecuted
n account of a protected ground , "
arr said .
n addition to ending asylum protections for most Central Americans ,
he rule is also likely to add a new hurdle for asylum-seekers
s they go through legal proceedings
o remain in the U.S. ,
s it would require officers and immigration judges " to apply this new bar on asylum eligibility " during " credible-fear " interviews ,
ccording to the text of the rule .
he rule applies only to those
ho enter or arrive in the U.S. on or after the rule ' s effective date ,
hich is expected to be Tuesday .
he move is likely to face legal challenges .
he American Civil Liberties Union promised to `` swiftly '' counter the new restrictions in court shortly after the rule 's announcement .
The Trump administration is trying to unilaterally reverse our country 's legal and moral commitment
o protect those fleeing danger , ''
ee Gelernt , the deputy director of the ACLU 's Immigrants ' Rights Project said in a statement .
his new rule is patently unlawful
nd we will sue swiftly . "
entral Americans make up the bulk of non-Mexican migrants
ttempting to enter the United States through the Southwest border
o claim asylum ,
ut recent years have seen an increase in prospective asylees from the Caribbean , South Asia and Sub-Saharan Africa .
he announcement is the latest of several efforts by the Trump administration
o tighten asylum rules at the border .
he president has railed against asylum-seekers ,
nd announced earlier this year
hat he would seek to adopt a series of restrictive measures
imed at those
oping to obtain asylum within the U.S .
onday ' s announcement also landed
fter immigration raids took place across the nation this weekend .
he ACLU filed a preemptive lawsuit ahead of the raids
imed at protecting the rights of asylum-seekers within the United States .
arr earlier this year issued an order
hat would allow
or certain asylum-seekers to be detained indefinitely during immigration proceedings .
ut a federal judge in Seattle struck down that rule ,
inding it unconstitutional .
he Ninth Circuit Court of Appeals is now hearing briefings on the appeal of the order .
he administration also implemented a " Remain in Mexico " policy earlier this year
hat requires
hat some asylum-seekers stay in Mexico
hile their cases are processed .
district judge in California initially ruled against the policy
nd blocked it from going into effect ,
ut the Ninth Circuit has since allowed it to be enforced during the appeals process .
rump has for months decried U.S. immigration laws as the " dumbest " and " worst " of any country in the world .
e has repeatedly called on Congress to tighten asylum laws in particular ,
ut legislation
o do so
as failed to gain traction in either chamber .
awmakers late last month passed a funding package
hat included $ 4.5 billion in resources for agencies
esponding to the influx of migrants at the southern border .
pprehension numbers surged in the first half of 2019 ,
ith a sharp decrease coming in June .
cting Department of Homeland Security
DHS )
ecretary Kevin McAleenan said in a statement Monday
hat the additional funding was insufficient on its own ,
nd that the interim rule will " reduce overwhelming burdens " on the immigration system in the meantime .
he rule announcement comes on the heels of visits to the border by several lawmakers and Vice President Mike Pence
